programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A woman is killed in a carjacking incident, but Horatio becomes convinced the seemingly random crime is actually a premeditated murder. Meanwhile, Alexx discovers Dr Monroe is drinking heavily - and declares her intention to turn him in. Holly Valance guest stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The teams fly to Los Angeles from Hawaii in pursuit of suspect Jarrod Taylor, before tracking him down at his hotel, where he claims he is being paid by an immunologist who is hoping to reintroduce smallpox to restore nature's balance. Later, the agents make a shocking discovery when they find a bulk order for children's t-shirts. Concluding part of the crossover episode with Hawaii Five-0.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray.(n)

Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Camille Guaty.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah performs a spiritual cleansing in the local creek as a religious occasion approaches, but she's spotted by Doris Collins. She finds it hard to deal with the woman's reaction to her nudity, but it is her own personal demons that she is struggling with the most. Marta Dusseldorp and Deborah Kennedy star.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Anna and Gino's romance continues to blossom as they sneak off to spend time together - even though it's clear that something about their secret trysts is playing on Gino's mind. Meanwhile, at Ash Park, Olivia and James's relationship is at breaking point as Olivia struggles with the identity of James's lover. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry appears in court to testify against brother Ben, but reacts badly to being questioned about his own childhood abuse, indirectly jeopardising the outcome of the trial. Phil begins to realise he is powerless to stop former lover Christine emigrating with their daughter Madison, while Smithy spends the night with sultry Louise Larson, unaware Zain Nadir is investigating the criminal activities of her husband Pete.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie is devastated when her patient collapses and there is not enough time to arrange a heart transplant. Nick tries to get Diane on his side by letting her perform a difficult procedure for the first time, while Ric talks her into taking the research position full time. Kyla brings Max to work as he is a diabetic and she is worried about his blood sugar levels.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The atmosphere is tense between new nurse Barney Wolfe and Adam as they fail to see eye to eye. Two serious cases of food poisoning panic one unfortunate fellow, who rushes home to check on his son, only to discover that his wife is having an affair, and Duffy begins to feel the pressures of her new responsibilities. Derek Thompson, Cathy Shipton and Ian Bleasdale star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Anita Dobson guests as Phil Hunter's mum Lynne, who turns up at the station to lecture him on his responsibilities as a father-to-be. Smithy seizes an opportunity to meet local villain's wife Louise Larson and continue their dangerous relationship, while Adam struggles to make headway with his road safety campaign, until old friend Isaac Collins pulls a few strings at the local council to help get it under way. In court, the case against Terry's brother Ben concludes - but the outcome promises to have a devastating long-term effect on the entire family.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Shelley's ready to be more than friends with Mark. Pauline's determined to find out about Pete's disappearance. Gita warns Cindy about Richard.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Richard continues to pursue Cindy, meanwhile Pauline has concerns about Arthur.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: A man climbs a chimney in protest against the Thames Relief Road, but the rescue escalates into a serious operation.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Archie begins coaching the Glenbogle five-a-side team for their big match against Glencampbell, but reluctantly hands over the reins to his brother Paul to allow him to concentrate on important estate business. Molly attempts to avoid a weekend trip with Andrew, fearing she is not ready for a new romance, and Golly is reunited with his estranged daughter Jess.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The nurses get more than they bargained for as they prepare for a darts match in the Skelthwaite Arms, and Beth has a surprise visitor. Drama, starring Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash.(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: The Boswell family try to cope without Nellie, who has some surprise visitors - her 'fancy man' Derek and Lilo Lill.(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: It is a day of discontent at Maplin's when the pay packets arrive, and Ted and Spike act out the French Revolution in the swimming pool. Comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean and Lionel settle down to enjoy a quiet evening, only to find themselves constantly disturbed by ominous noises from the empty house next door. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo gets out his old motorbike and tries to perform a few stunts - much to the surprise of Blamire and Clegg. Bill Owen, Michael Bates and Peter Sallis star.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective duo are called to a hospital, where a series of suspicious deaths has raised fears a serial killer may be at work on the wards. A grisly collection of body parts is found in a nearby lake, and both patients and staff are dropping dead with alarming regularity. However, Pascoe soon finds his own health is as much of a worry as the case itself. Robert Powell, Miriam Karlin and Haydn Gwynne guest star.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Fresh DNA tests on an unidentified body found in 1996 reveal a family link to a Home Office fingerprint analyst, who has not seen her brother since she was seven, when he brought her over to the UK from their home country of Albania. The victim had been working as a police interpreter - and the motive seems clear when it turns out his last assignment was translating for a witness in a murder trial against a notorious Albanian criminal. Meanwhile, Gerry embarks on a French cookery course, but the language barrier results in a rather surprising date. Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Eve links DNA evidence found at a traffic collision to a murder case she worked on while examining mass graves during the Balkan War, leaving Boyd determined to track down the perpetrators, a ring of war criminals now living and working in the UK. Starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston, with guest stars Ron Cook, Anna Madeley and Branka Katic.(n)

programme.desc: Sarah heads to Sydney with George and Anna to see her ill mother, while Elizabeth decides to reveal her darkest secret in the hope that it will save James and Olivia's marriage. Elsewhere, Sir Henry Swanson makes a request to receive a Japanese trade delegation at Ash Park.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth is rushed to hospital after suffering a heart attack, and is looked after tenderly by Jack, but comes close to revealing a secret from her past while heavily sedated. Her frail health makes Anna and Gino decide to postpone telling their families about their relationship, but Carla becomes suspicious after finding contraception hidden in Anna's room.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Anita Dobson guests as Phil Hunter's mum Lynne, who turns up at the station to lecture him on his responsibilities as a father-to-be. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Elliot fears for his wife, who has motor neurone disease and whose health appears to be getting worse. He also regrets their decision to keep her condition a secret from Martha - not realising she knows something is up and has persuaded Joseph to help her learn the truth. Sam uncovers a family secret when he treats a boy who is undergoing a heart transplant.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Sean asks Max to put in a good word for him with Tina, but the result is totally unexpected. Holly befriends a little boy caught in the crossfire of marital breakdown, and a pensioner with a fractured neck sparks recriminations between her husband and son. Gerald Kyd and Claire Goose star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. Gina returns to her job immediately after receiving treatment for her cancer. Clearly unfit for work but too stubborn to admit it, she relies on June to oversee the relief. Gary is shot during a raid on a gang suspected of handling stolen goods, while Phil bristles with righteous indignation at the discovery that Kate has been mugged at knifepoint and vows to catch the thug responsible. As for Adam, he hits another snag as he tries to get his road safety initiative up and running, and again asks Isaac Collins to help - unaware his old friend's shady past is about to catch up with him.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy is going to the restaurant with Richard. Kath is fed up with Arthur and Mrs Hewitt. Dot visits her grandson. Gita tells her sister she has to go.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy confesses to Ian that she has been out with Richard. Dot visits her grandson Ashley.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: The Blue Watch boys tackle two different, but equally dangerous and challenging blazes, one at a car-breaker's yard, the other in a Chinese restaurant.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Dorothy announces her plans to build an eco-dome, but Lexie is suspicious of her motives - and when it is revealed the proposed access road runs through the newly established outdoor activity centre, relations break down between Paul and Archie. Meanwhile, Duncan relishes his new job but discovers Hermione has lied to him, and Jess accuses Golly of being sexist when she fails to get the head ranger's job.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: David puts his money worries aside to captain the local cricket team in a grudge match against their rivals _ but is the game up for the bungling businessman? Hussan uses a cheap trick to try to win Jess back, while Anna refuses to let Simon help her with the baby, determined to go it alone, but finds comfort in sharing her woes with young mum Marie Briggs, who is also struggling to cope with the pressures of single parenthood. Small-town drama, starring Lesley Dunlop and Philip Middlemiss.(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Nellie, newly released from hospital, meets the family at the Victorian mansion for their holiday - but things aren't exactly relaxing.(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Gladys agrees to marry Clive - much to his family's dismay. Comedy, starring Ruth Madoc.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean begins to think that, 19 months after tying the knot, her marriage may have become stale. She wonders whether a few surprises from Lionel would spice things up, so Alistair, helped by Judith and Sandy, comes up with a plan to give her the treat of a lifetime.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Cyril Blamire leaves to live in Oswestry, after hearing a lady he is interested in has become a widow. However, his old friend Foggy Dewhurst arrives in the village, and soon makes his presence known to the locals. Brian Wilde stars.(n)

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The judge hears the case of three men accused of shooting a rival gang member - but the trial is in danger of collapsing when the prosecution's main witness is killed in a hit-and-run incident. To make matters worse, the jurors start to fall sick, and the newly appointed Home Secretary sees a chance to make it into a jury-less trial - which could force Deed to throw it out altogether. Martin Shaw stars, with Jenny Seagrove.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: New information links the unexplained death of a market trader to a series of drug rapes in east London - but with that case still ongoing, Ucos officers are unable to question the chief suspect, so they have to look into their victim's past in their search for clues. Gerry finds a likely culprit in the shape of the market inspector who clamped his car, while Sandra has problems of a more personal nature when her hated mother arrives for a visit. Sheila Hancock guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After a naked woman is found fleeing a car accident, she is taken to hospital and tests reveal her DNA is linked to a double murder in 1960s Soho. Boyd discovers the victim, successful entrepreneur Elizabeth Andrews, has suffered a horrific attack at the hands of her abductors and he is led to her home, where a man presumed to be her husband is found hanged in the hallway. With a new murder as well as the cold case to solve, the team tries to connect the crimes, but a greater danger awaits Elizabeth in hospital. Drama, starring Trevor Eve and his real-life wife Sharon Maughan.(n)

programme.desc: Sarah and George begin a secret affair and meet at the cottage. However, Anna hopes that if she can push her father towards Sarah that he will be more open to learning about her relationship with Gino, but she is frustrated over the arrival of her aunt Regina. Meanwhile, James struggles with his emotions. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is asked to host a Japanese trade delegation on the eve of Remembrance Day. Knowing how the community will react, he asks Jack to come to the reception in the hope that the approval of a former Japanese prisoner of war might help deal with any ill feeling. At the event Regina is openly hostile toward Sarah, which she attributes to anti-Semitism. Anna's prolonged absence from Ash Park has Gino fearing that their relationship is over, but he is unaware that she is with Caroline awaiting the results of a pregnancy test. Meanwhile, James and Olivia continue to grow closer. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. Gina returns to her job immediately after receiving treatment for her cancer. Clearly unfit for work but too stubborn to admit it, she relies on June to oversee the relief. Gary is shot during a raid on a gang suspected of handling stolen goods, while Phil bristles with righteous indignation at the discovery that Kate has been mugged at knifepoint and vows to catch the thug responsible. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The tension between Ric and Nick reaches crisis point during a bowel transplant operation, and Diane is forced to pick sides. Joseph treats a Bosnian woman who does not speak English, and is unaware of the full extent of her condition - but his decision to tell her the truth has disastrous consequences. After dealing with a medical emergency in the lab, Matt realises he misses treating patients.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The arrival of arrogant new registrar Patrick Spiller makes a bad impression among staff, particularly Holly, who has crossed his path before. Adam receives a nasty reminder of Sam's accident, and the parachute jump proves eventful for a nervous Max. Claire Goose and Robert Gwilym star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Price to Pay - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. Jack embarks on a relentless search for the gunman who shot Gary - and soon realises the finger of suspicion is pointing firmly at Adam's friend Isaac Collins. Smithy and Jonathan call at Gina's house to see why she hasn't turned up for work, and discover her unconscious on the bathroom floor. Away from the line of duty, Tony feels dejected at not having a date for Honey's birthday party and decides to hire an escort for the evening - little anticipating the surprise awaiting him.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Richard gets an unwelcome surprise from Cindy and Ian. Dot is in a happy mood as she looks forward to a special visitor. Nigel receives some bad news from Debbie via Clare.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Beth breaks Nigels weak and feeble heart and Michelle entrusts Shelly with Vicky, only to find out that shes exposed her daughter to a drug fuelled pot session.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Bluewatch are called to extinguish a fire at a school and find two missing boys. Meanwhile, Adriane falls down a flight of stairs during an argument with Nick.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Archie has to choose between giving his business partnership with Paul one last chance or going mountaineering in Nepal with his sister Lizzie to honour their father's memory. Meanwhile, Molly is angry that Andrew has ignored her since returning from their romantic weekend, but Golly gains the courage to admit his hidden feelings to her. Alastair Mackenzie and Susan Hampshire star.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Tom Beresford returns to Skelthwaite 20 years after a bitter family row, lured by the offer of a managerial position at Goddard's, and it's not long before he and his good-looking son are setting tongues wagging. Karen breaks all the rules to help two families come to terms with the consequences of a transplant operation, but her ill-advised actions backfire. Vic decides the time has come for a younger man to captain the Scorpions and an emotional Simon proposes to Anna. Drama, starring Tony Haygarth, Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash.(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Connie and Oswald join the holiday to see Billy and Aveline, and Joey's protest about the game shooting on the estate wins him an admirer.(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy becomes a Yellowcoat, but her happiness is spoilt when Alec Foster arrives with bad news. Last-ever episode of the hit comedy, starring Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean calls on Alistair's help to do a spot of match-making and Lionel finds himself in the middle of a muddle. Comedy, with Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The mischievous trio come to the aid of an old friend with an unusual medical problem, while Ivy becomes increasingly irate that no one will fix the cafe roof.(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently investigate a consignment of stolen passports, but the case takes an unexpected turn when a woman linked to the crime is found dead on the seashore with her mixed-race baby lying next to her. As the duo follow a lead on the murder victim's ex-boyfriend, they encounter racial prejudice and are drawn into an underground world of gang wars within the Arab community. Primeval's Andrew-Lee Potts guest stars.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. But when the team question the victim's boyfriend and her fellow backpackers, they find themselves with very little to go on. Meanwhile, JP prepares for the biggest day of his life as his wedding day approaches, and when Humphrey bumps into an old lady friend, Florence encourages her boss to pursue his quest for love.(n)

programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers. Here, the team at one of the country's biggest shopping malls installs a three-metre tall reindeer for Christmas shoppers. In Tynemouth the biggest fish catch of the year is landed with only hours to process the catch, while refuse collectors work hard to get Newcastle's streets clean and waste recycled before dawn breaks.(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining 16 contestants are split into two groups as they compete for the chance of their first taste of working in a professional kitchen. The first eight cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace, with the best five heading to Da Terra in east London to work under chef Rafael Cagali. During a busy lunch service, the amateurs will be entirely responsible for serving the restaurant's menu to paying customers. Will any buckle under the intense pressure as they cook in a demanding new environment?(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Colin Salmon narrates a documentary exploring how the lives of humans and animals across a vast area of the planet are affected by the transforming power of the monsoon rains. The end of the dry season in northern Australia sees 300,000 flying foxes gathering to drink while running a gauntlet of freshwater crocodiles, while in Sumatra a female orang-utan guides her youngster to where she knows a fig tree fruits at this time of year. At the Delhi stock exchange, the price of rice escalates every day the rains fail to materialise and priests submerge themselves in water and beseech Varuna, god of water, to come.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Ian and Toby from Essex and Charley and David also from Essex compete for a chance to meet today's curious collector Charlie. With Â£1,000 at stake in the Curiosity Shop, who will curate the most valuable collection? Paul Martin hosts.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel the country to examine the UK's obsession with teatime treats. They make Bakewell tart in its original Derbyshire home and prepare a Victoria sandwich for the ladies of the WI. At Henley regatta, the duo prepare cookies with sour cherries and chocolate chips, and later serve afternoon tea to the Tees Rowing Club.(n)

programme.desc: Steve Davis v Jimmy White. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the 1984 World Championship final, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott takes Len Goodman on a road trip around Britain to expand the Strictly Come Dancing judge's culinary horizons. The pair begin in Manchester, where Len has his first taste of Chinese food and samples some Mexican mezcal, while Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a stir-fried beef dish inspired by the day's adventures.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts David Harper and Claire Rawle search for items to sell at auction at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales. Presented by Paul Martin.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Amanda Abbington, Michael Buerk, Shappi Khorsandi and Elis James test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Poultry farmers Nick and Jacob and organic and heritage fruit and vegetable farmer Patrick display their wares at Alexandra Palace Farmer's Market.(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: Part one of two. wildlife expert e explores the unique scenery of the Brecon Beacons over the course of a year, braving the elements in each season to detail the ever-changing landscape of the valleys. He begins in the winter enduring a raging blizzard on the high peaks, while the lowlands are bathed in sun. Later, nesting is in full swing as spring arrives and while hundreds of dotterel are resting in the Black Mountains during their journey from Africa to breeding sites in Scotland, peregrines are nesting in an old quarry in the Central Beacons.(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the fifth semi-final of the quiz, inviting four returning contenders to brave the black chair once more. They answer questions on the specialist subjects of the Trans-Siberian Railway, Dusty Springfield, the Monty Python films and Grayson Perry, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge.(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as the competition reaches its climax after getting under way last July, when 28 teams entered the contest with high hopes. They were gradually whittled dow to the final two after a grand total of 36 first- and second-round matches, then the quarter- and semi-finals. The two teams will be endeavouring to succeed last year's winners the University of Edinburgh and become the 49th champions of the quiz, carrying off the trophy designed by Manchester sculptor Adrian Moakes.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: The Restaurant That Burns Off Calories
programme.desc: Maitre d' Fred Sirieix and doctor Zoe Williams open a restaurant with a difference, welcoming 20 unsuspecting diners for a slap-up meal. It all sounds normal enough, but at the back of house is a fully functioning gym, where a group of fitness fanatics are poised on exercise bikes, treadmills and rowing machines, ready to burn off every single calorie ordered and consumed by the diners. This experiment is based on scientific research that suggests that people eat 20 percent less when shown how much effort it takes to remove excess calories.(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: According to one report, the three most stressful experiences in life are the death of a spouse, divorce, and imprisonment. Moving house is only 32nd on the list, but anything can happen in this tale involving the Handle Me Gently removals company and a nervous resident. Written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Emilia Fox, Monica Dolan and Nick Moran.(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Scott traces the history of Sicily, from the first arrival of ancient Greek settlers 3,000 years ago to the arrival of the Byzantine Empire. He begins his journey on the slopes of Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano, and discovers how the Greeks began to settle on Sicily's east coast - planting their olives and vines and building great city states that soon came to rival even Athens itself.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Making a living in farming today is hard work and taking big risks can help a farm survive. Here, three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity, but only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards held in London. Narrated by Denis Lawson.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile

Plus, Adam Henson is on his farm finding out about the pecking order of his chickens.(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Big Fat Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. Actress Miriam Margolyes travels around the nation to discover why so many people are overweight, despite so much information about healthy lifestyles. She wants to understand what impact being obese has on people and how the rest of society views them and begins by meeting people fighting back against their size - both physically and psychologically.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Leather expert Suzie Fletcher takes a look at an old piano stool, which has been handed down through four generations of a family. Elsewhere, experts Richard Biggs and Dominic Chinea turn their attention to restoring a toy bus and a pair of binoculars.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: The effect of the rainy season on wildlife in Asia, with floods forcing animals to flee from Kaziranga National Park in north-east India in search of the safety of the hills. In Cambodia, the Mekong river swells so much that water is forced back up one of its tributaries to fill the vast Tonle Sap lake - one of the most productive freshwater fisheries on Earth. Fresh grass draws nomads and their vast herds of livestock back to their homelands, but they are stalked by hungry wolves and hyenas, which move in at night for the kill. Colin Salmon narrates.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After a frantic race to make it to Ilha Grande, the teams set off for the next checkpoint in Mendoza, Western Argentina, but as the race enters its final phase, the depleted budgets begin to take their toll on the competitors. Emon and Jamiul are the first to set off, but disagree about the best route to take. Jen and Rob are the team with the most money remaining, but also have clashing priorities over how best to proceed. Jo and Sam look for jobs that include bed and board to save their money, while Dom and Lizzie hitch a ride and receive an unexpected invitation.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison come up with virtual reality designs to transform a Halifax couple's 19th-century home. When owner Paula developed MS, it became vital to make the house more wheelchair accessible - but her partner Jeremy also sees an opportunity to give it a stylish new look. Laura's concept makes the most of the views of the garden and uses clever recycling tricks to stretch the budget, while Robert deploys off-the-shelf materials to making the place more accessible.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a soup factory in Wigan that produces 2.5 million cans a day, and follows the production of a tin of vegetable soup from a pea harvest to the eventual dispatch. He also encounters a pressure cooker that stands 27.4 metres tall. Cherry Healey compares the vitamin content of fresh and frozen vegetables, and visits a salt mine in Crewe, while historian Ruth Goodman cooks up a butterted spinach soup using a recipe from a 17th-century cookbook, which is the earliest known reference to soup in English.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Kelly Osbourne, Ade Edmondson, Sara Pascoe, Jermaine Jenas and Nicola Coughlan play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Emily Maitlis presents analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: The Restaurant That Burns Off Calories
programme.desc: Maitre d' Fred Sirieix and doctor Zoe Williams open a restaurant with a difference, welcoming 20 unsuspecting diners for a slap-up meal. It all sounds normal enough, but at the back of house is a fully functioning gym, where a group of fitness fanatics are poised on exercise bikes, treadmills and rowing machines, ready to burn off every single calorie ordered and consumed by the diners. This experiment is based on scientific research that suggests that people eat 20 percent less when shown how much effort it takes to remove excess calories.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones consider three more money-making proposals. The ideas under deliberation are an environmentally friendly range of plastic-free water bottles made from starch, ergonomic underwear, and an armchair tray for elderly people. Evan Davis presents.(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the world's tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the state's changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely.(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Big Fat Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. Miriam Margolyes sets out to find out what is being done to combat the obesity crisis, and meets people who are going to extreme and sometimes controversial lengths to lose weight. To understand the true cost of the epidemic, Miriam meets 41-year-old Angela, whose leg was amputated two years ago. Her weight meant she needed a knee operation, which led to a sepsis infection. Stuck at home relying on carers to wash, dress and feed her, Miriam is shocked to learn how Angela's weight upended her life.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles presents footage of an angler fishing on a rock who is swept into a raging sea, and a mountain biker whose attempt at a stunt does not go according to plan.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion oversees proceedings in the north-west of England as experts David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams buy their three items to take to auction.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: As Knockout Week continues, the second group of eight amateurs compete for the chance to cook in a professional kitchen for the first time. First, the contestants are challenged to create a dish for the judges that they would have on the menu of their own restaurant, pop-up or feature in their cookbook. After this challenge, three of the group are sent home, and the remaining five head to Manchester to work in the flagship restaurant of 2015 MasterChef Champion, Simon Wood.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: The other side of the monsoon, as the winds bring dry air and drought. Budgerigars swarm in the Australian desert, gathering at a precious waterhole, and although birds of prey try to ambush them, they are mesmerised by the array of bright colours on show. On the island of Borneo, a bearded pig hunts for seafood on the beach, while in the Rajasthan desert, nomads haggle over the price of camels, which can provide vital transport during this lean season. The mugger crocodiles of Sri Lanka stalk the animals taking a much-needed drink and a young leopard learning to hunt discovers there is no such thing as an easy meal. Colin Salmon narrates.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as teams from Devon and east London put their knowledge of antiques to the test, while competing to build the most valuable collection, for a chance to win Â£1,000.(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Short
programme.desc: Simon King and David Myers take a trip through Romania, where they visit the imposing Corvin Castle and cook pork sarmales by the drawbridge.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Steve Davis v Joe Johnson. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the 1986 World Championship final, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len has his first taste of Middle Eastern food and samples Peruvian ceviche in central London. Later, Ainsley takes inspiration from the day and heads to the kitchen to cook chicken shawarma.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From the Boconnoc estate in the Cornish countryside, antiques experts Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall uncover a pair of Georgian chairs and a children's pedal fire engine.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Shappi Khorsandi, Michael Buerk, Amanda Abbington and Elis James test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Former police officer Huw Murphy and the Harries family go up against each other with their beloved shire horses at the 37th South Wales Shire Horse Society Show.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of southern Spain, travelling from Valencia to Torremolinos and finding out about the influence of the Moors, who invaded the country in the early eighth century. Along the way, they visit one of the most fertile areas in Spain, get their hands on a freshly caught octopus, go for a mud bath and learn about flamenco, giving Dave a chance to show off his dance moves. Finally, they cook tapas for the San Juan midsummer night festival, where they reflect upon their entire Mediterranean adventure.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Guy Owen, Joe Baker, Thomas Carr and Harriet Mansell compete in the heats for the South West. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including Wind in the Willows, Enid Blyton and Jeremy Fisher.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Katie is annoyed that Devs is being misused for the amusement of Lyndon and Stewart, despite Forest and herself being willing to break the rules. On a flying visit to the 'Amaya' campus, Senator Laine warns Forest that he will face questions before an oversight committee. Meanwhile, concerns about Lily's state of mind are escalated, and Kenton tries to allay her fears, while being reassured by what he learns. Elsewhere, Jamie aims to make himself useful. Mystery thriller, starring Sonoya Mizuno and Jin Ha.(n)

programme.title: Mister Winner
programme.desc: Leslie, his best friend Cooper, father-in-law Chris and his mum's boyfriend Steve all go kayaking for his stag do. Paired up with Chris, Leslie suggests they take a short cut in an attempt to beat the others to the pub for lunch, but they soon find themselves lost in the middle of a huge lake, without a clue which direction they should head in.(n)

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Sketch show, with Freya Parker and Celeste Dring, featuring characters from their live shows, including adventures of a whimsical French heroine, created by a man who has never met a woman.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.sub-title: Stacey Dooley: Locked Up With the Lifers
programme.desc: The presenter visits a women's prison in America that has adopted a radical new approach to incarceration based on giving the inmates a sense of purpose and self-esteem. She meets eight prisoners who are serving life sentences for murder, and discovers the complex sexual relationships that have developed among many of the inmates.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates his painting technique as he creates tranquil scenes taken from nature, beginning with mountains, evergreens, paths and bushes.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.sub-title: Medieval Maps - Mapping the Medieval Mind
programme.desc: Cartography through the ages, beginning with the Hereford Mappa Mundi, the largest intact medieval wall map in the world, which was intended to picture all of human knowledge in a single image. The programme traces its history, and features interviews with people who have been affected by it, including Turner Prize-winning artist Grayson Perry.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Romans with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The classicist takes a look at the history of the ancient city, putting aside the bloody stories of emperors and armies to learn about the ordinary Roman on the street. Her travels take her from the Via Appia roadway, originally built in 312 BC, to the Colosseum and Monte Testaccio, an artificial hill made of broken crockery. Along the way, she tells the stories of a slave-turned-baker who made a fortune out of the grain trade, building his tomb in the shape of a giant bread-oven, three prisoners of war and a purple-dye seller whose raw material was imported from Tunisia.(n)

programme.title: Da Vinci: The Lost Treasure
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce travels to Florence, Milan, Paris and Warsaw to tell the story of Leonardo da Vinci's life, exploring why a man whose reputation rests on only a few pictures - including the Mona Lisa - is regarded as one of the greatest painters of all time. She also visits New York to see a recently discovered piece by the artist.(n)

programme.title: Patrick Kielty's Mulholland Drive
programme.desc: Patrick Kielty's journey to find the truth about Belfast-born William Mulholland, who was responsible for building Los Angeles' water infrastructure and providing a water supply that allowed the city to grow into one of the largest in the world. Patrick's family all worked for the water company in Northern Ireland and upon discovering that the man who gave LA its water was from Belfast, decided to find out why he isn't globally recognised.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.sub-title: Knights of the Road: The Highwayman's Story
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores the criminal underworld of Britain in the 17th and 18th centuries, beginning with the arrival of the highwayman in the wake of the English Civil War. Heavily romanticised in literature, these glamorous gangsters became a social menace on the roads and a political thorn in the side of the creaking British state, but a far darker reality lay underneath the dashing image of stylish robbers on horseback.(n)

programme.title: The Search for the Lost Manuscript: Julian of Norwich
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez tells the story of the first book written in English by a woman, Revelations of Divine Love by Julian of Norwich. The book was written in 1373 and presents an alternative viewpoint on man's relationship with God, which led to it being suppressed for more than 500 years. She travels from Norwich to Cambrai in Northern France, revealing how the book survived and the stories of women who championed it, and consults with academics and theologians about the book's importance.(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.sub-title: The Secret Life of Books: Swallows and Amazons
programme.desc: Broadcaster John Sergeant takes to the water to explore the origins of Arthur Ransome's children's classic about the adventures of a group of children.(n)

programme.title: Life Is a Circus
programme.desc: Documentary following a group of homeless people in Oxford over the course of a year, as they come together to devise and stage an original piece of theatre.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates his painting technique as he creates tranquil scenes taken from nature, beginning with mountains, evergreens, paths and bushes.(n)

programme.title: Da Vinci: The Lost Treasure
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce travels to Florence, Milan, Paris and Warsaw to tell the story of Leonardo da Vinci's life, exploring why a man whose reputation rests on only a few pictures - including the Mona Lisa - is regarded as one of the greatest painters of all time. She also visits New York to see a recently discovered piece by the artist.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an American south western mountain scene, with red peaks looming over a lazy, tree-lined stream.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.sub-title: London: City Maps - Order Out of Chaos
programme.desc: The series heads for the British Library to look at maps of London, examining how representations of the city have changed over the course of three centuries. Among the documents examined are maps of the capital after the devastating fire of 1666, a neo-Classical interpretation of the city from 1746, and a map depicting sites of personal significance produced by contemporary cartographer Stephen Walter.(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how scientists exploited the natural world to make medicines, beginning by investigating the challenges they faced trying to create a drug that would alleviate pain. The programme examines what physical discomfort actually is, why people want to control it, and how the discovery of morphine led to 200 years of scientific breakthroughs and self-experimentation.(n)

programme.title: Expedition Volcano
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following scientists studying some of the world's most dangerous active volcanoes in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The series begins by exploring Nyiragongo, which has erupted twice in the past 50 years, most recently in 2002, when it devastated the nearby city of Goma. However, it lies in a region troubled by war and humanitarian crises, which has made study of the volcano difficult. Former royal marine Aldo Kane is given the task of ensuring the team's safety.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.sub-title: Secrets of Orkney: Britain's Ancient Capital
programme.desc: Andy Torbet dives below the waves in search of the inspiration for the first stone circle, while Chris Packham and Neil Oliver spend the night on an abandoned island as they hunt for clues as to why cultures change. Meanwhile, Shini Somara tests the technology behind a bronze age sauna and the archaeologists uncover a remarkable find.(n)

programme.title: The Marvellous World of Roald Dahl
programme.desc: Documentary making use of archived letters, writings and biographical material to tell the story of the life of Roald Dahl, in his own inimitable voice. From his time as a fighter pilot in the Second World War, through to his work as a spy and his encounters with Walt Disney and Theodore Roosevelt, the programme explores how Dahl's personal experiences may have informed the fantastical tales he would eventually publish, including Charlie and the Chocolate Factory and Matilda. Includes contributions by Dahl's last wife Liccy, his daughter Lucy and biographer Donald Sturrock. Long-term collaborator Quentin Blake also provides new animated illustrations based on the events of Dahl's life.(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak challenges received wisdom about the cultural movement that began in the Late Medieval period. He begins by examining the notion of the Renaissance having fixed origins in Italy, showcasing the ingenuity in both technique and ideas of great artists including Van Eyck, Memling, Van der Weyden, Cranach, Riemenschneider and Durer.(n)

programme.title: The Art That Made Mexico: Paradise, Power and Prayers
programme.desc: Alinka Echeverria explores the three major forces that have shaped Mexican art, as well as the country itself, beginning by exploring the influence of nature and land.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an American south western mountain scene, with red peaks looming over a lazy, tree-lined stream.(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how scientists exploited the natural world to make medicines, beginning by investigating the challenges they faced trying to create a drug that would alleviate pain. The programme examines what physical discomfort actually is, why people want to control it, and how the discovery of morphine led to 200 years of scientific breakthroughs and self-experimentation.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross takes on one of his greatest challenges as he paints majestic mountains looming over a colourful seascape in only half an hour.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: Paul Barber of the British Library discusses the golden age of Dutch cartography and examines the importance of atlases to the commercial and creative endeavours of the 17th century. As maps became a means of expressing power, wealth and control, they grew increasingly elaborate and artistic - and the documentary visits the world's largest atlas, a 24-volume example covering just the Netherlands, and the Klencke Atlas, made for Charles II's restoration in 1660.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the tale of the village of Kibworth in Leicestershire, detailing its status at various points in English history. He begins by examining the village's roots, using archaeology, landscape, language and DNA to create an impression of its first 1,000 years. He enlists the aid of local residents to dig 55 test pits, and excavates artefacts from the Roman era through to the Middle Ages.(n)

programme.title: King Arthur's Britain: The Truth Unearthed
programme.desc: With access to a major new excavation on the Tintagel peninsula in Cornwall, Professor Alice Roberts pulls together the latest evidence to reveal what Dark Age Britain was really like. Long known to have been a Dark Ages settlement, the new evidence reveals that Tintagel was also a seat of power, and Alice explores the link between the location and Arthurian legend, tracking down the early sources for the period and the first written reference to King Arthur.(n)

programme.title: Archaeology: A Secret History
programme.desc: Richard Miles charts the history of archaeological breakthroughs in a mission to understand the ancient past. In the first programme, he explores how the profession began by trying to prove a biblical truth.(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Historian Janina Ramirez and art critic Alastair Sooke embark on cultural city breaks, offering insights into the most famous locations and uncovering hidden gems and untold stories to reveal how religion, revolution and trailblazing individuals can shape the art - and soul - of a metropolis. They begin by examining the art and design of the Dutch capital Amsterdam, along the way discovering how its culture reflects the liberal attitudes, appetite for global trade and love of home comforts that helped shape the character of this city.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Wally is missing and Elwood City's best detective is called upon. However, that detective is Binky and Fern is determined to prevent him getting the case.(n)

With a brand new channel and nothing to put on it, Larry then makes the classic mistake of employing Diddy Dick and Dom to take charge of programming. However, his reactions leave her convinced that something is wrong, but does not think anyone will believe her. She does not realise that the man in the cell is one of Omar's followers, who has taken his place so the terrorist leader can plan a major attack.(n)

programme.desc: Dougie Vipond explores the impact of Covid-19 on farming, while Anne Lundon is the first of Landward's presenters to reveal her favourite stretch of coastline.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany berates Keegan for his bad mood and the chaos at the Taylors' only adds to his stress, leading to an angry outburst - but he is mortified to realise Bailey has witnessed his violent. Sonia urges Whitney to get back to normal, but that proves easier said than done with Michaela posting disagreeable online comments online, leaving her crushed. Ash cries on Vinny's shoulder but they both panic when Suki arrives home early, and after speaking to Callum, Stuart is inspired to propose to Rainie again.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Bake Off judge Prue Leith, reality TV star Scarlett Moffatt, rapper Big Narstie and weatherman Tomasz Schafernaker join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A student backpacker is found dead in the showers of her hostel, and with a gun in her hand and her body slumped against the only door, all evidence points toward suicide. Humphrey isn't so sure, however, especially when he notices all her toiletries laid out and her towel missing. But when the team question the victim's boyfriend and her fellow backpackers, they find themselves with very little to go on. Meanwhile, JP prepares for the biggest day of his life as his wedding day approaches, and when Humphrey bumps into an old lady friend, Florence encourages her boss to pursue his quest for love.(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Poppy investigates a new line of enquiry that threatens to have severe implications on Caitlin's trial. Elsewhere, Jessie takes control as the Murdoch boys get in each other's way and Scarlett lies to ensure Bob helps with a local initiative.(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie struggles with being placed on reduced duties, while some difficult sessions with a psychiatrist force her to confront some home truths. Fearing that her military career is over, she steals Antonio's security pass so that she and Prof can confront Omar in his cell. However, his reactions leave her convinced that something is wrong, but does not think anyone will believe her. She does not realise that the man in the cell is one of Omar's followers, who has taken his place so the terrorist leader can plan a major attack.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: Documentary revealing what it really takes to deliver Britain's next generation through the eyes of parents and staff at three of Birmingham's busiest maternity hospitals. The programme goes beyond the labour ward to meet parents at every stage of their pregnancy, capturing the work of midwives, doctors and surgeons dedicated to supporting families as they bring new lives into the world. In the first episode, Channelle wants an intervention free birth, Jodie's baby arrives eight weeks early, and there is joy for Ashleigh after a dramatic delivery.(n)

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Yung Filly comes to the aid of a bartender who has never had a girlfriend, giving him the chance to snoop around the homes of three potential partners.(n)

programme.title: Addicted: America's Opioid Crisis
programme.desc: Documentary exploring America's prescription opioid addiction crisis, which has led to 130 fatal overdoses a day, as well as being a gateway drug to heroin. The programme examines the pharmaceutical company Purdue Pharma, owned by the philanthropic Sackler family, which has made billions from the sale of the drugs, and questions why medical regulators and government agencies have not done more to prevent the scale of the addiction crisis.(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Eve attempts to rebuild her life, having been shot and left for dead in Rome by Villanelle, who is approached by an old foe who offers her a new opportunity. Kenny tries to unearth information about the Twelve, and Carolyn is undermined at work. The return of the spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer and Sandra Oh.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush continues to hide Tommy's secret and while dropping him off at school, he asks Isaac to keep an eye on him. Denise is stunned to learn what happened to Keegan and Jean receives a gift from Daniel, leaving her touched.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: Documentary revealing what it really takes to deliver Britain's next generation through the eyes of parents and staff at three of Birmingham's busiest maternity hospitals. The programme goes beyond the labour ward to meet parents at every stage of their pregnancy, capturing the work of midwives, doctors and surgeons dedicated to supporting families as they bring new lives into the world. In the first episode, Channelle wants an intervention free birth, Jodie's baby arrives eight weeks early, and there is joy for Ashleigh after a dramatic delivery.(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie struggles with being placed on reduced duties, while some difficult sessions with a psychiatrist force her to confront some home truths. Fearing that her military career is over, she steals Antonio's security pass so that she and Prof can confront Omar in his cell. However, his reactions leave her convinced that something is wrong, but does not think anyone will believe her. She does not realise that the man in the cell is one of Omar's followers, who has taken his place so the terrorist leader can plan a major attack.(n)

programme.title: Addicted: America's Opioid Crisis
programme.desc: Documentary exploring America's prescription opioid addiction crisis, which has led to 130 fatal overdoses a day, as well as being a gateway drug to heroin. The programme examines the pharmaceutical company Purdue Pharma, owned by the philanthropic Sackler family, which has made billions from the sale of the drugs, and questions why medical regulators and government agencies have not done more to prevent the scale of the addiction crisis.(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Eve attempts to rebuild her life, having been shot and left for dead in Rome by Villanelle, who is approached by an old foe who offers her a new opportunity. Kenny tries to unearth information about the Twelve, and Carolyn is undermined at work. The return of the spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer and Sandra Oh.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, craftsman David Burville takes on the repair of a little clockwork tractor that holds significant memories for owner Nicola Clemo, as it belonged to her father. Meanwhile, Sandy Richards from Cornwall arrives, bearing a precious but delicate memento of her beloved parents for the attention of ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay, a plate bearing a photograph of Sandy's mother. Elsewhere, a military compass and an old vibraphone are restored by experts Barry Phillips and Pete Woods.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett welcomes twelve more contestants to the amateur sewer competition. First up, the sewers are challenged to create seemingly straightforward wardrobe staples, and for their pattern challenge, the judges ask them to make a wrap skirt. Finally, the contestants attempt to make their first made-to-measure garment, a tea dress.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to turn a tower of jelly into art and make a magnet-powered motor. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.desc: Following the activities of young Eboo, an elephant in the Amboseli National Park - which is overlooked by Mount Kilimanjaro.(n)

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes view of Barra Airport, the only place in the world where scheduled flights land on the beach. It is a busy time for Avril Irvine in Shetland, and the Sumburgh firefighters are busy dealing with birds, marshalling planes and training for disaster.(n)

programme.title: Kerry is Kirsty
programme.sub-title: Kerry is Kirsty: Morar gu Arasaig
programme.desc: Cycling sisters Kerry and Kirsty MacPhee cycle the nation's favourite cycle routes. Starting on the banks of Britain's deepest loch, Loch Morar, in Lochaber they reach the coastal road at Arisaig with stunning views across to the small isles.(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee present the best of the action from the second day of the Scottish National Sheep Dog Trial in Brisbane Glen Estate, West Kilbride. The weather holds, but it's a difficult course for the handlers. A variety of sheep means it's the luck of the draw what packet the handlers get, with some being more stubborn than others. Good runs from Jimmy Menzies and Ross Henderson put them in a good position to make the team.(n)

programme.title: Mach Ã Seo!
programme.desc: A young person's guide to the Scottish staycation, in which Ramsay MacMahon banishes those weekday blues and proves that a week's worth of living can be crammed into the weekend. Alan Titchmarsh, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney give Elijah the sensory stimulation he craves, transforming the family's uneven overgrown urban courtyard into an accessible paradise complete with tropical water feature, a sensory beach and Asian-style dining zone.(n)

programme.title: A Mother's Son
programme.desc: First of a two-part drama, starring Hermione Norris, Martin Clunes, Paul McGann and Alexander Arnold. The murder of a schoolgirl rocks the residents of a quiet coastal town in Suffolk, and when Rosie stumbles upon a pair of bloodstained trainers hidden in her home, she suspects someone in her family may have been involved in the killing. With her son Jamie and stepson Rob both acting suspiciously, could she be right?(n)

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix head to San Francisco, the free-spirited capital of the United States. They start out by sampling the latest trends of using cannabis in cooking, which was legalised in California in 2018 - but as they enjoy a hippy afternoon tea, Gino overindulges and chaos ensues, especially when the next activity on the agenda is a session of goat yoga. Moving on, the trio dig for clams on the Pacific coast, take a hot-air ride to a vineyard and visit a factory in Chinatown where fortune cookies are made. It seems a competition is in order and so the travel companions see who can make the most cookies in 60 seconds.(n)

programme.title: A Very British Lockdown: Diaries from the Frontline
programme.desc: Documentary featuring a wide cross-section of people as they record their own stories on camera phones - charting the everyday courage, kindness, anxiety and humour they experience as the nation strives to 'Keep Calm and Carry On'. From shopkeepers to home-schooling parents, care workers to cleaners, teachers to nurses, the film reveals what HM The Queen recently called 'our quiet, good humoured resolve'. Others - such as expectant Mums and the sick and elderly - show their bravery in the face of the coronavirus.(n)

Councillor Bone is sure their relationship is a front, but that does not stop a good old-fashioned show down at the pub. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Jimmy Akingbola and Barbara Flynn.(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: The conclusion of Julian Fellowes' drama about secrets and lies in uptight 19th-century London society. James travels to Manchester to investigate the allegations made against Charles, but soon realises there are greater threats at play. Lady Templemore employs Reggie to make Lady Maria change her mind, but her daughter will not give in so easily. Susan is in danger of losing of losing everything after he secret is revealed, but as ever she used her cunning to twist the situation to her own and others' benefit, while Charles is puzzled to receive a mysterious olive branch. Keen to end the feuding, he seeks out the offer of reconciliation - unaware he is being lured into danger.(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster takes in the delights of the Lancaster Canal, setting out from Tewitfield. As he meanders down the waterway, John cannot resist visiting Carnforth railway station, where he re-enacts his favourite scene from the classic film Brief Encounter, some of which was filmed there. After Carnforth, the canal crosses the River Lune on its way to Lancaster, where the veteran journalist marvels at the engineering of the Lune Aqueduct. Later, he heads south to Garstang, famous for its cheeses, and finally on towards Preston.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a hyperactive staffie called Cookie, who training staff believe could make an excellent working dog, but this is not usual for staffies, so finding someone to take him on may be difficult. Paul also meets a two-year-old Dogue de Bordeaux in need of some love after being found abandoned in a park, and nine-month-old teacup poodle Charlie is having trouble running with her broken leg. Head vet Shaun explains that Charlie has three breaks on one leg, and the best thing for her is going to be to remove it.(n)

programme.title: Our Queen at War
programme.desc: How the life of the young Princess Elizabeth was shaped by the events of the Second World War, and the extraordinary experiences she went through during her teenage years. It seems a competition is in order and so the travel companions see who can make the most cookies in 60 seconds.(n)

programme.desc: Documentary featuring a wide cross-section of people as they record their own stories on camera phones - charting the everyday courage, kindness, anxiety and humour they experience as the nation strives to 'Keep Calm and Carry On'. From shopkeepers to home-schooling parents, care workers to cleaners, teachers to nurses, the film reveals what HM The Queen recently called 'our quiet, good humoured resolve'. Others - such as expectant Mums and the sick and elderly - show their bravery in the face of the coronavirus.(n)

programme.desc: The conclusion of Julian Fellowes' drama about secrets and lies in uptight 19th-century London society. James travels to Manchester to investigate the allegations made against Charles, but soon realises there are greater threats at play. Lady Templemore employs Reggie to make Lady Maria change her mind, but her daughter will not give in so easily. Susan is in danger of losing of losing everything after he secret is revealed, but as ever she used her cunning to twist the situation to her own and others' benefit, while Charles is puzzled to receive a mysterious olive branch. Keen to end the feuding, he seeks out the offer of reconciliation - unaware he is being lured into danger.(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster takes in the delights of the Lancaster Canal, setting out from Tewitfield. As he meanders down the waterway, John cannot resist visiting Carnforth railway station, where he re-enacts his favourite scene from the classic film Brief Encounter, some of which was filmed there. After Carnforth, the canal crosses the River Lune on its way to Lancaster, where the veteran journalist marvels at the engineering of the Lune Aqueduct. Later, he heads south to Garstang, famous for its cheeses, and finally on towards Preston.(n)

programme.desc: Lois gets a job as a postal worker to pay for Stewie's private school and finds an unsent letter from Peter that was meant for an old flame of his.(n)

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys win 100 denarii each after their bet on a race pays off and it looks as if their fortunes might have taken a turn for the better. Stylax decides to reinvent himself as a charioteer, while Marcus prefers to spend his money on romantic pursuits. Starring Tom Rosenthal, Joel Fry and Ryan Sampson.(n)

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Marcus is unexpectedly asked to be best man for the upcoming wedding of Aurelius, so he sets about organising the stag night. Meanwhile, Stylax takes chariot lessons from a volatile driving instructor, and Grumio gets caught up in Landlord's drug dealing. Comedy, guest starring Shaun Williamson (EastEnders, Extras).(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma and Audrey spend a day shopping in Southport. Samir and Tracy have a counselling session with the consultant. Steve invites Fiona on a business trip to Ireland.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily thanks Samir for being selfless in giving Tracy his kidney. Ken is stunned when Denise tells him she wants to start afresh.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is worried that he failed to impress Linda. Betty takes advantage of Eric's emotional state. Kim wants to know what Dave sees in Kathy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nellie fusses over Sam on his first proper day of work. Dave has a surprise in store for Kathy. Nellie and Zak's romantic meal out descends into chaos.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol asks Joe for assistance when suspicions mount against her following the death of a pensioner who she frequently visited. Peggy is annoyed to find a pile of rubbish has been dumped in her backyard and Gina shares her concerns with Carol as she approaches the end of her pregnancy.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park Cricket Club prepare for the opening ceremony of their new pitch and pavilion, Mim arranges a big surprise for Rog - but disaster is waiting in the wings. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger gets a frosty response when he introduces new team member Clive to the others, while Kev and Mag present Mim with a motorised lawn-mower. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.sub-title: The Last of the Big Spenders
programme.desc: Rigsby invests in some new furniture in a desperate attempt to impress Ruth's replacement, Brenda, but makes the mistake of opting to pay for it on the never-never. Leonard Rossiter and Gay Rose star.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Two new residents put PC Mason in an awkward position both professionally and personally and, while the village rallies round to celebrate Blaketon's birthday, he gets more than he expected when Gina goes into labour. David decides to learn to play the piano. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire. Starring Joe McFadden, Tricia Penrose and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave and Kathy tell Betty about their engagement. Nellie and Zak aren't on speaking terms, and Linda makes a shock announcement.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is devastated about what she knows about Dave and Kim. Danny doesn't believe he is the father of Linda's child, and Terry's happy hour doesn't take off at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel picks on Tricia for always being late to work. Samir suggests to Deirdre that they should stay in Weatherfield after the operation, and Reg forces Maureen into work.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet warns Steve not to involve Vicky in any dodgy deals. Deirdre warms to the idea of staying in Weatherfield, and Reg takes Tricia on at the shop.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Two new residents put PC Mason in an awkward position both professionally and personally and, while the village rallies round to celebrate Blaketon's birthday, he gets more than he expected when Gina goes into labour. David decides to learn to play the piano. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire. Starring Joe McFadden, Tricia Penrose and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: In the summer of 1967, Morse and Thursday are both devastated by Joan's abrupt departure from Oxford. The young detective responds to his loss by throwing himself into his work, investigating the suspicious drowning of a scientist who was part of a research team working on an early computer. At first it appears to be a simple suicide - until two more bodies are found in the river.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Conclusion. The mysterious death of serial killer Marcus Payne makes the task of finding his accomplice more difficult and holds up the search for Leanne Wray. With time against him and DS Annie Cabbot breathing down his neck, Banks follows a hunch and takes Payne's wife in for questioning on suspicion of involvement with the rape and murder of the four girls. The lead she provides is more startling than anything the detectives are prepared for. Drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Charlotte Riley and Andrea Lowe.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple

Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan and Philip goad Rigsby into fasting for three days - and it is not long before he is stealing Vienna's cat food to stave off the pangs of hunger. With Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale.(n)
