programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Rog gets busy organising the team's first match, while Mim tries to cheer up Bob after Ginnie gives him his marching orders. Maggie's sister inadvertently lets slip a secret - with devastating consequences. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Kevin and Maggie have an awkward conversation about their fertility problems, while Rog and Mim arrive to discuss the forthcoming marriage blessing. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.sub-title: Things That Go Bump in the Night
programme.desc: Rigsby manages to frighten Alan by kidding him the house is haunted by a spectral 'grey lady' - but ends up getting a taste of his own medicine. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An encounter with a gang of armed men presents Miller with a difficult dilemma that puts his professional and personal future in jeopardy. Mason and Wetherby struggle to deal with student protesters who are furious at the presence of a suspected Nazi collaborator in the town, and the situation escalates further when an attempt is made on the man's life. John Duttine and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is worried when Linda is late for work. Kathy confronts Kim with what Dave has told her about their affair, and Linda takes an overdose.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan and Ned wait for news on Linda, who has been admitted to intensive care. Tina flirts with Vic in front of Viv, and Ned forces his way into the Weirs' house to confront Danny.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim feels unable to relax with the Rachids sharing his home. Betty gives Raquel dancing lessons, and Tracy suggests to Samir that they cancel the operation and wait for a donor.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf is stunned when he's awarded an OBE. Tracy meets Daniel for the first time, Maud warms to Tricia when she stands up to her, and Samir is attacked by a gang of youths.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An encounter with a gang of armed men presents Miller with a difficult dilemma that puts his professional and personal future in jeopardy. Mason and Wetherby struggle to deal with student protesters who are furious at the presence of a suspected Nazi collaborator in the town, and the situation escalates further when an attempt is made on the man's life. John Duttine and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.desc: Rigsby challenges Philip to a boxing match, convinced Miss Jones will throw herself at his feet if he wins. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger insists on leading the team convoy of cars to a fixture in the country but his map-reading skills let him down en route, and a run-in with a troublesome tractor driver ensures the day ends dismally. Richard Harris's comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Josie Lawrence and Timothy Spall.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Mim tries to persuade Roger to cancel a match with Piggy Pearson's team because of an incident with the latter's divorced sister. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby returns from a dismal holiday in Spain to find a surprise awaiting him - the love of his life, Miss Jones, is back - with a baby in tow. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A notorious burglar escapes from prison and returns to Aidensfield to pursue a vendetta against Ashfordly police. Roping in two local boys, the villain dupes them into helping him carry out his plan while PC Mason desperately tries to find him before he strikes again. Dawn sets Joyce up on a date with PC Younger - but is oblivious to the fact that the young copper actually has feelings for her. George Cole guest stars, with Joe McFadden and John Duttine.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is told by the doctors she is lucky to be alive. Kim is furious about Kathy being Joseph's godmother, and Tina announces a Save The Dingles fundraiser at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry is annoyed with Tina for not telling him about the fundraiser. Sarah wants Jack to apologise to Ned, and Nellie writes to John Major, the Queen and Terry Wogan for help.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police call on Deirdre and take her to the hospital. She is stunned when the doctors tell her Samir isn't going to recover, and she must give consent for the kidney to be taken.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy comes round from her operation. Deirdre doesn't know how to tell Samir's mother that he's dead, and Ken doesn't let Tracy know what's happened to Samir.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A notorious burglar escapes from prison and returns to Aidensfield to pursue a vendetta against Ashfordly police. Roping in two local boys, the villain dupes them into helping him carry out his plan while PC Mason desperately tries to find him before he strikes again. Dawn sets Joyce up on a date with PC Younger - but is oblivious to the fact that the young copper actually has feelings for her. George Cole guest stars, with Joe McFadden and John Duttine.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Friend of the Devil - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Cabbot investigates when a woman is found murdered on the moors. As the identity of the victim is established, it becomes apparent any number of suspects may have had a reason to want her dead. Meanwhile, Banks is called in when the body of a teenage girl is discovered in a storage room down an alleyway. Stephen Tompkinson and Andrea Lowe star.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Friend of the Devil - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective and his team find themselves investigating the murder of one of their own. Cabbot is convinced there is a link between the deaths of Lucy Payne and her colleague, but is dismissed as trying to push her career ambitions. However, after receiving new information from another force, she begins to uncover the real connection between the killings. Andrea Lowe stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: It's the day of Kevin and Maggie's wedding - but they're nowhere to be seen. As the members of the congregation fidget, Clive makes an accusation against Roger. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet warns Steve not to involve Vicky in any dodgy deals. Deirdre warms to the idea of staying in Weatherfield, and Reg takes Tricia on at the shop.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave and Kathy tell Betty about their engagement. Nellie and Zak aren't on speaking terms, and Linda makes a shock announcement.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is devastated about what she knows about Dave and Kim. Danny doesn't believe he is the father of Linda's child, and Terry's happy hour doesn't take off at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Two new residents put PC Mason in an awkward position both professionally and personally and, while the village rallies round to celebrate Blaketon's birthday, he gets more than he expected when Gina goes into labour. David decides to learn to play the piano. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire. Starring Joe McFadden, Tricia Penrose and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Rog gets busy organising the team's first match, while Mim tries to cheer up Bob after Ginnie gives him his marching orders. Maggie's sister inadvertently lets slip a secret - with devastating consequences. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Kevin and Maggie have an awkward conversation about their fertility problems, while Rog and Mim arrive to discuss the forthcoming marriage blessing. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.sub-title: Things That Go Bump in the Night
programme.desc: Rigsby manages to frighten Alan by kidding him the house is haunted by a spectral 'grey lady' - but ends up getting a taste of his own medicine. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An encounter with a gang of armed men presents Miller with a difficult dilemma that puts his professional and personal future in jeopardy. Mason and Wetherby struggle to deal with student protesters who are furious at the presence of a suspected Nazi collaborator in the town, and the situation escalates further when an attempt is made on the man's life. John Duttine and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is worried when Linda is late for work. Kathy confronts Kim with what Dave has told her about their affair, and Linda takes an overdose.(n)

programme.desc: Jan and Ned wait for news on Linda, who has been admitted to intensive care. Tina flirts with Vic in front of Viv, and Ned forces his way into the Weirs' house to confront Danny.(n)

programme.desc: Jim feels unable to relax with the Rachids sharing his home. Betty gives Raquel dancing lessons, and Tracy suggests to Samir that they cancel the operation and wait for a donor.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf is stunned when he's awarded an OBE. Tracy meets Daniel for the first time, Maud warms to Tricia when she stands up to her, and Samir is attacked by a gang of youths.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby returns from a dismal holiday in Spain to find a surprise awaiting him - the love of his life, Miss Jones, is back - with a baby in tow. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A notorious burglar escapes from prison and returns to Aidensfield to pursue a vendetta against Ashfordly police. Roping in two local boys, the villain dupes them into helping him carry out his plan while PC Mason desperately tries to find him before he strikes again. Dawn sets Joyce up on a date with PC Younger - but is oblivious to the fact that the young copper actually has feelings for her. George Cole guest stars, with Joe McFadden and John Duttine.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is told by the doctors she is lucky to be alive. Kim is furious about Kathy being Joseph's godmother, and Tina announces a Save The Dingles fundraiser at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry is annoyed with Tina for not telling him about the fundraiser. Sarah wants Jack to apologise to Ned, and Nellie writes to John Major, the Queen and Terry Wogan for help.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police call on Deirdre and take her to the hospital. She is stunned when the doctors tell her Samir isn't going to recover, and she must give consent for the kidney to be taken.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy comes round from her operation. Deirdre doesn't know how to tell Samir's mother that he's dead, and Ken doesn't let Tracy know what's happened to Samir.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A notorious burglar escapes from prison and returns to Aidensfield to pursue a vendetta against Ashfordly police. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Friend of the Devil - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Cabbot investigates when a woman is found murdered on the moors. As the identity of the victim is established, it becomes apparent any number of suspects may have had a reason to want her dead. Meanwhile, Banks is called in when the body of a teenage girl is discovered in a storage room down an alleyway. Stephen Tompkinson and Andrea Lowe star.(n)

programme.title: Tommy Cooper
programme.desc: Actress Diana Dors joins the incomparable comedian as he performs a variety of sketches and tricks. The ensemble cast features Peter Reeves and Clovissa Newcombe.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A newly appointed coroner faces a baptism of fire as local community figures call for his resignation almost as soon as he takes up the post.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo receives death threats from an ex-convict with a vendetta - but he is not the only one in danger, as the vengeful crook targets the detective's niece on her wedding day.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar finds a suspicious building in Wales which is being used as a research laboratory, plunging him into the midst of a spine-chilling adventure. Spy drama, directed by and starring Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: A Number of Old Wives' Tales
programme.desc: Apparently successful entrepreneur Clive Cosgrove loses his 'confidence' after Arthur and Terry attend his wedding celebrations. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Mower, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is faced with a terrifying campaign of explosions and assassinations, apparently committed by an opposing operative that Cowley is convinced he killed several years earlier. It appears that he is either mistaken or an old associate of his former adversary is seeking revenge. Gordon Jackson stars.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. The plot thickens when illicit cocaine is retrieved and arms stolen from the US are discovered to be on their way to a military faction in Ireland. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner is forced to set aside his scalpel and rush to an Arizona Indian reservation to help his foster son Chester, who is suffering from bubonic plague.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo's niece is kidnapped on her birthday by criminals who, demand the release of their accomplice, who is in custody for killing a police officer. Guest starring Richard Gere.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates the suspicious deaths of five company directors, who each received a card with the words 'The Scales of Justice' printed on it prior to their demise. However, he finds his inquiries frustrated by the remaining members of the board. Roger Moore stars.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry is assigned to mind a writer of romantic fiction with a drink problem, whose ex-husband has sold their house behind her back. Beryl Reid and Bill Maynard guest star, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are pitted against KGB assassins when a CI5 operative is apparently raised from the dead. A proposed exchange finds Cowley at a deserted aerodrome, and a burst of gunfire apparently leaves his life on the line. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A former criminal and friend of Regan's wins a large amount of money on the pools - but then receives blackmailing phone calls regarding a crime from 11 years earlier for which he was never caught. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Edward Judd, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.desc: Part two of two. Jeremy Wade takes on the biggest investigation of his career - to unravel what happened to the passengers of the RMS Laconia in the Atlantic.(n)

programme.desc: Despite his reputation for finding big-money hauls, skipper Peter finds pickings slim at the end of the winter season. Elsewhere, the Minnamurra is heading back to port as Clive plans to motor through the night to try and capitalise on the current high lobster price.(n)

programme.title: Can Science Beat the Virus?
programme.desc: A special debate about the coronavirus pandemic, with only scientists on the panel. As Britain increasingly looks beyond politics to science for reassurance about how and when the crisis will end, this programme brings together experts to take part in a straight-talking, fact-based discussion providing scientific answers to the most difficult questions. The discussion covers the psychology of isolation, the technical issues behind mass production of ventilators, and, crucially, gives viewers the chance to explore the fascinating science behind this extraordinary outbreak.(n)

programme.desc: Actor Tom Conti, mercurial gardener Diarmuid Gavin, ex-athlete Tessa Sanderson, singer Tony Christie and presenter Penny Smith explore and enjoy life in the slow lane aboard two barges, narrow-boating along two of France's best-loved waterways - the River Lot and the Canal du Midi. After meeting each other and moving into their boats, the usually unflappable Penny needs rescuing after tangling with a tree. Tom takes charge of their mini-fleet, but gets into a scrape of his own, while below deck Diarmuid targets Tony for some gentle ribbing, while Tessa resents being bossed about.(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor sets off from Seattle on an Amtrak Cascades train into Canada, where he is struck by the beauty of Vancouver and the warmth of the locals. He then takes a two-day rail journey aboard the Rocky Mountaineer. While the other passengers relax and enjoy the luxury service, Tony is put to work in its kitchen. Lastly, he travels via Alaska to the small town of Talkeetna. Boarding a float plane, Tony flies over snow-covered mountains to reach the base of the Alaskan glaciers, where he witnesses the impact of climate change up close.(n)

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain. He begins in the cab of Puffing Billy, the world's oldest-surviving steam locomotive, which was built in 1813 and designed to move coal along a five-mile stretch of track from a Northumberland mine to the docks. Chris also tells the story of Henry Booth, who not only championed George Stephenson's famous Rocket, but also helped finance it, and talks about the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: Liverpool cathedral is the Church of England's newest cathedral, and the largest in Britain. It was designed in the Gothic style by Giles Gilbert Scott, who also designed the red telephone box. It took 74 years to build and was paid for by money donated from the people of Liverpool. National tragedies such as the sinking of the Titanic - many of whose crew came from Liverpool - and the Hillsborough football disaster are remembered within its walls. It is also where Paul McCartney composed and performed his first classical piece of music, the Liverpool Oratorio.(n)

programme.desc: Tom Conti, Diarmuid Gavin, Tessa Sanderson, Tony Christie and Penny Smith meet British artists Jeff and Sally, who fell in love with France's River Lot and decided to stay and spend their lives painting it. Plus, Tom and Tony visit a cave system, only discovered in the last century, to view some 30,000-year-old art. At a typically vibrant French market in the valley, Diarmuid hunts for snails - preferably ones steeped in garlic and butter - while Tom demonstrates his acting prowess with a drunken sailor routine.(n)

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: How dogs are trained to use smell for life-saving conservation work and environmental policing. Featuring a South African dog helping to monitor endangered tortoises, a labrador who ensures that non-native zebra mussels do no damage to the habitats of Western Canada, and a whale scat detection dog, who plays a vital role in research into the decline of orca whales.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following Julian Norton and Peter Wright. Peter gets an emergency call from sheep farmer Clive, whose ewe has gone into labour, but the lambs are tangled up inside their mother. One arrives safely but the second isn't breathing, and Peter's skills are put to the test when he realises there is a third lamb still to come. In Boroughbridge, Julian and Anne operate on a dog with a tumour in its chest.(n)

programme.title: A Country Life for Half the Price with Kate Humble
programme.desc: Kate meets a family from Essex who have bought four acres of woodland in Suffolk to escape the growing development and noise pollution of their Rochford home. They plan to develop bird photography and beekeeping businesses, while a vegetable garden offers the opportunity to become completely self-sufficient.(n)

programme.title: Fred & Rose West: 5 Clues That Caught the Killer
programme.desc: The infamous crimes of Fred and Rose West, who carried out a series of murders at their home on Cromwell Street, Gloucester between 1967 and 1987. Deputy senior investigating officer Terry Moore reveals the details of the police work that resulted in their arrests and ultimately the conviction of Rose West, with Fred committing suicide while on remand in 1995.(n)

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell heads to Norfolk to follow a lost railway through some of the quietest - yet wealthiest - parts of the country. This railway was a curious latecomer when it was built in the 1880s and it threatened to rudely thrust the region into the industrial age. Rob explores an entire engineering town built from scratch, learns how Norfolk's agriculture was super-charged, and sees for himself where thousands of holidaying Midlanders arrived to sail the Norfolk Broads. The line put Norfolk on the frontline of the war so Rob takes to the skies to spot dozens of hastily constructed airfields.(n)

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.sub-title: How the Railways Won the War
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines the role of the railways during the two global conflicts of the 20th century, keeping vital supply lines open and transporting troops to and from the front. In the First World War, railway works were converted into munitions factories and women were employed for these dangerous jobs and their heroic efforts helped win them the vote, while in the Second World War, they were vital in organising the evacuation of a million children and offered underground shelters from air raids.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor recounts the history of Winchester Cathedral, the longest medieval cathedral in northern Europe, which has witnessed civil wars, religious battles and struggled with collapsing foundations. It is the burial place of novelist Jane Austen - though only four people attended her funeral and originally her plaque made no mention of her writing. Tony learns that Winchester's bishops were among the richest, most influential and most badly behaved in the country and some were wealthier and more powerful than even England's kings.(n)

programme.desc: Colby is rushed to hospital in agony, where he undergoes emergency surgery, while Justin wants to know about Leah's ordeal, but dreads hearing what she may reveal.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Harlow is furious with Paul for deleting Prue's voicemail, and they clash over Roxy's idea for a memorial. Shane has doubts about accepting an accelerated course at university.(n)

programme.title: Killer Best Friend (2019)
programme.desc: Best friends Katie and Jess find their relationship tested when Jess begins a new relationship and an unhealthy obsession consumes Katie. Thriller, starring Mary Grill.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Cry
programme.desc: Phoebe accidentally becomes a porn icon, Rachel tries to stop her sister from embarking on a relationship with Ross, and Chandler reveals he is unable to cry. Guest starring Reese Witherspoon.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One That Could Have Been - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A revelation from Rachel prompts the pals to ponder what might have been if their lives had taken different courses. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox.(n)

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Harlow is furious with Paul for deleting Prue's voicemail, and they clash over Roxy's idea for a memorial. Shane has doubts about accepting an accelerated course at university.(n)

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: Penny Smith, Tony Christie, Tessa Sanderson, Diarmuid Gavin and Tom Conti tackle a new waterways adventure on the Canal du Midi, a 17th-century feat of engineering. They find their new boats at the marina in Sete on the coast of the Mediterranean. The banter begins with their first decision over who gets which boat, resulting in repelling of boarders, cauliflower cannonballs and the confiscation of keys. The famous five tangle with local fishermen and have confused communication with bridge controllers, but eventually leave port and set off across France's largest lagoon, the Etang du Thau.(n)

programme.title: Sewermen
programme.desc: Workers have three weeks to clean an effluent-encrusted sewer beneath a golf course, unless the rain comes, while Denzil the sniffer dog detects a whiff of chlorine in the Welsh borders. A potential pollution problem must be fixed within two hours, or heavy fines will be incurred.(n)

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: A patient comes into the surgery for a further examination after a prostate cancer screening, and a woman suffering from pain after the removal of a contraceptive coil is treated. Elsewhere, Dr Qamar has an appointment with teenager Laila, who has been experiencing painful headaches on a daily basis.(n)

programme.title: Casualty 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of medical emergency teams in the A&E department of Barnsley Hospital. Paramedics rush in Dean, a 30-year-old who has been admitted after being in a serious motorbike collision and may have spinal injuries. Elsewhere, doctors treat a 20-year-old with severe abdominal pain, and a 90-year-old with an injured finger.(n)

programme.title: And Proud
programme.desc: Documentary following a group of new arrivals from their Roma village to London, originally shown to coincide with EU work restrictions being lifted. Members of Roma families already established in the UK also talk about their experiences.(n)

programme.title: Millionaire Age Gap Love
programme.desc: Part two of two. The stories of multimillionaire celebrity hair stylist Nicky Clarke, who unexpectedly found love with his PA Kelly Simpkin, and 72-year-old millionaire Lyn, who is accustomed to dating men 40 years her junior and is on the hunting for Mr Right.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: Comedy about a family of deaf gangsters who protect their interests with their signing hands. When the opera family threaten their dominance a fingerspelling war ensues.(n)

programme.title: Time Bandits (1981)
programme.desc: A bemused schoolboy is escorted on a bizarre journey through time by a group of larcenous dwarfs. His adventures lead him to encounters with various characters from history - but none of them are quite what he had imagined. Terry Gilliam's fantasy comedy, starring Craig Warnock, David Rappaport, John Cleese, Michael Palin, Sean Connery, Ian Holm, Ralph Richardson and David Warner.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Cabby (1963)
programme.desc: The neglected wife of a workaholic taxi owner grows increasingly tired of his lack of interest in their marriage. In a ploy to bring him to his senses, she secretly launches a rival cab firm with a driver roster made up of attractive women, who soon tempt his customers away. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Connor and Charles Hawtrey.(n)

programme.title: Hulk (2003)
programme.desc: Scientist Dr Bruce Banner turns into the rampaging green Hulk after exposure to a huge dose of gamma radiation, prompting the military to embark on a no-holds-barred mission to destroy the monster. Meanwhile, Banner's unstable father resurfaces, holding the secret to his transformation. Comic-book adventure from director Ang Lee, starring Eric Bana, Jennifer Connelly, Sam Elliott and Nick Nolte.(n)

programme.title: Exodus: Gods and Kings (2014)
programme.desc: Moses, the adoptive brother of the pharaoh of Egypt, is sent into exile, where he receives a message from God demanding he set all the country's slaves free. When his brother refuses to comply, the kingdom is struck by a series of devastating plagues. Biblical epic, starring Christian Bale, Joel Edgerton, Sigourney Weaver and John Turturro.(n)

programme.title: The Last Stand (2013)
programme.desc: Disgraced narcotics cop Ray Owens retires into a quiet life as sheriff of a small town on the Mexican border. He gets the chance to prove his worth as a law enforcer when a drug lord escapes from prison and tries to flee the US - with his escape route taking him through Ray's jurisdiction. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Johnny Knoxville and Forest Whitaker.(n)

programme.title: T2 Trainspotting (2017)
programme.desc: Recovering heroin addict Mark Renton returns to his Edinburgh roots after 20 years living overseas, hoping to make peace with the friends he abandoned. However, starting anew proves far from easy, especially when old partner in crime Begbie escapes from prison, still brooding on Renton's betrayal. Danny Boyle's drama sequel, starring Ewan McGregor, Robert Carlyle, Ewen Bremner and Jonny Lee Miller.(n)

programme.title: The Rise (2012)
programme.desc: A criminal under police interrogation openly admits his part in a robbery that ended in an attempted murder. He tells the inspector handling the case the story of how his reunion with three friends following his release from prison led to his part in the crime, and how their actions were driven by revenge. Crime drama, starring Luke Treadaway and Timothy Spall.(n)

programme.title: Our Man in Havana (1959)
programme.desc: A vacuum cleaner salesman is recruited as an agent by British intelligence, but struggles to cope with the demands of the role and starts to fabricate information to keep himself gainfully employed. Carol Reed's satirical spy drama, based on Graham Greene's novel, starring Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Maureen O'Hara, Noel Coward and Burl Ives.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Constable (1960)
programme.desc: A flu epidemic leaves a police station short of staff, forcing a desperate inspector to send four bungling trainees on the beat. The inept would-be bobbies manage to increase the crime rate even before they have started work, and their long-suffering sergeant faces an uphill battle to whip them into shape. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims.(n)

programme.title: Funeral in Berlin (1966)
programme.desc: Former crook and disreputable spy Harry Palmer has to carefully engineer the defection of a Russian officer responsible for safeguarding vital military secrets - but the mission proves less than straightforward. Cold War thriller and the second film to feature Len Deighton's character, starring Michael Caine, Eva Renzi, Oscar Homolka, Paul Hubschmid and Guy Doleman.(n)

programme.title: Young Sherlock Holmes (1985)
programme.desc: The teenage detective and his future sidekick Watson meet for the first time at boarding school. The death of Holmes's mentor sets the youngsters on their first case, and they discover that former members of an archaeological survey team have died under suspicious circumstances - pointing to a sinister Egyptian cult using hallucinogenic poisons to kill its victims. Adventure, starring Nicholas Rowe, Alan Cox and Sophie Ward.(n)

programme.title: Europa Report (2013)
programme.desc: A privately funded spaceship with an international crew is sent to investigate the potential of Jupiter's fourth largest moon to support life, but technical glitches and human error plunge the astronauts into extreme danger. Found footage sci-fi thriller, starring Daniel Wu, Sharlto Copley and Michael Nyqvist.(n)

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul.(n)

programme.title: Sabotage (2014)
programme.desc: The members of an elite Drug Enforcement Administration team secretly keep the millions of dollars they find during the raid. When they are killed off one by one, the leader becomes convinced one of his men is getting rid of the others to keep all the money, and sets out to expose the murderer. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger and Sam Worthington.(n)

programme.title: Lore (2012)
programme.desc: A German teenager raised by Nazis is left to look after her younger siblings after her parents are imprisoned in the last days of the Second World War. As she leads her family across the war-torn country, she is helped by a Jewish refugee and starts to question the beliefs her mother and father taught her. Drama, starring Saskia Rosendahl and Nele Trebs. In German and English.(n)

programme.title: Houseboat (1958)
programme.desc: An Italian socialite rebels against her domineering father and gets a job as a maid for a widowed father. While helping her employer learn to understand his three precocious children, she also falls in love with him and must decide whether or not to reveal her secret. Romantic comedy, starring Cary Grant and Sophia Loren.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Nurse (1959)
programme.desc: The patients on the men's ward of Haven Hospital cause more mayhem for the staff than the rest of the hospital combined, forcing the management to send in a formidable matron to control the situation. Feeling aggrieved by her no-nonsense methods, the chaps decide the time is right to get their own back. Comedy, with Kenneth Williams, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey, Shirley Eaton and Leslie Phillips.(n)

programme.title: All That Heaven Allows (1955)
programme.desc: A wealthy New England widow breaks all the taboos of her conservative social circle by falling madly in love with her younger gardener. Her friends and grown-up children are scandalised as she contemplates marrying him, leaving her with a dilemma - to either bow to convention or take a chance at being happy. Romantic drama, with Jane Wyman, Rock Hudson, Agnes Moorehead and Conrad Nagel.(n)

programme.title: Baggage Claim (2013)
programme.desc: An air hostess is horrified at the thought of her younger sister getting married while she is still single. She realises her job gives her access to eligible men from across America, and begins clocking up thousands of air miles as she scours the country for a potential fiance. Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs.(n)

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy with facial disfigurements that have so far required almost 30 operations takes on a whole new challenge when, after being home educated his entire life, enters mainstream school for the first time. As he struggles to fit in, his fellow pupils and the community are forced to learn the power of compassion and acceptance. Julia Roberts, Owen Wilson and Jacob Tremblay star in Stephen Chbosky's drama, based on the best-selling novel by RJ Palacio.(n)

programme.title: Midnight Run (1988)
programme.desc: When accountant Jonathan Mardukas skips bail after having been arrested for embezzlement, bounty hunter Jack Walsh is hired to catch the fraudster and return him from New York to Los Angeles. Unfortunately, his task is hindered by the ingenuity of Mardukas and the attentions of pursuing FBI agents and Mafia hitmen. Comedy thriller, starring Robert De Niro, Charles Grodin, Yaphet Kotto, Dennis Farina, John Ashton and Joe Pantoliano.(n)

programme.title: Ashby (2015)
programme.desc: As part of a school assignment, student Ed Wallis gets to know his neighbour Ashby Holt, an ex-CIA assassin with a personal secret. Comedy drama, with Mickey Rourke, Nat Wolff, Emma Roberts and Sarah Silverman.(n)

programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel begins a relationship with a younger man, while Lennox faces a challenge from one of her classmates when she takes up the post of editor of the school blog. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Mike books a doctor's appointment after Molly realises he has not had one in more than 10 years, and badgers him to get checked out. Starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.desc: Penny's father pays a visit and Leonard discovers she told her parents they were back together, so he plays his boyfriend role to the hilt - much to her dismay. Meanwhile, Raj gets drunk and makes a pass at Howard's girlfriend Bernadette. Guest starring Keith Carradine.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy finds she has romantic feelings for Penny's former boyfriend Zack, while Raj and Howard begin competing to prove which of them would make the better superhero. Comedy, guest starring Brian Thomas Smith.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly reignites a long-standing feud after she invites Murray's unreliable brother to visit at Thanksgiving. programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica's new job at the department store is put in jeopardy when Beverly takes advantage of her daughter's employee discount too frequently. programme.desc: Captain Holt's mother seeks support from the precinct when her house is burgled. Jeffords, Diaz and Boyle redecorate the breakroom. Santiago teaches Gina how to change a tyre. US cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Andre Braugher.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Meanwhile, Raj's father comes to visit, and the guys do their best to free a bird they accidentally let into the university clean room. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: In order to achieve a better work-life balance, Mel agrees to put Joe in charge of renovating her home - but his management style leaves a lot to be desired. Meanwhile, Lennox's school drama club decides to stage a play she has written. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.sub-title: If You Can't Stand the Heat
programme.desc: Mel is charmed by the man who designs her new kitchen cabinets, but Joe fails to see eye-to-eye with him - leading to a big argument. Meanwhile, Lennox reluctantly attends one of her school's student committees, before an encounter with the organisation's presidents bolsters her enthusiasm. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Kay moves in with Peggy and it seems her passion and drive have departed, but Molly is determined to bring back the fun.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago try to get their parents to bond over Thanksgiving dinner, but their first holiday together as a family doesn't go well. Meanwhile, at the precinct, Captain Holt and Kevin's special Thanksgiving pie is stolen from Holt's office, so he hunts through the ranks for a possible culprit. Cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Melissa Fumero, with guest appearances from Jimmy Smits and Katey Sagal.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jeffords' favourite author, DC Parlov, gets in touch after his latest manuscript is stolen, so Jeffords and Peralta convince Diaz to go undercover with them at a fantasy fiction convention to find the culprit. Back at the precinct, Holt, Santiago and Boyle take a forensics course, but Boyle's big mouth gets him in trouble.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard finds out that he has a half-brother, Sheldon and Amy have a platonic sleepover, and Penny gets an offer to audition for a Kevin Smith film. American comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj is asked to create a message on the off-chance a Nasa mission discovers alien life, and the guys fight over what he should do. Meanwhile, Penny contemplates returning to acting. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco and Kunal Nayyar.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Astronautical engineer Adam and junior doctor Steph kick the week of soirees in and around Berkshire. The next hosts, stockbroker Simon and Liz, go for a gastropub-style menu, but not all of their guests are fans of their ingredients. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Diaz work and rework a crime scene in their efforts to solve a difficult murder case that is clouded by confusing evidence. However, their sympathy for the mourning mother of the victim leads them to make unrealistic promises to her.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Inbetweeners

programme.desc: Mike and Carl both ask to be assigned different partners at work, while Molly and Peggy have an opportunity to promote their new book on national radio. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Partridge in Oxfordshire, where young couple Matthew Cockman and Paula Szkwarkowska hope guests will get on board with their passion for local organic food. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Strawberry Gardens B&B in Heysham for the third day of the contest, where husband and wife team Susan and David Speak offer home-from-home accommodation and an abundance of northern hospitality. Sports enthusiast David treats his guests to a traditional darts competition, adjudicated by former world champion Alan Warriner-Little, but he is unimpressed when Paula sends back her evening meal. However, it is Chris and Jacqui's feedback that disappoints the hosts the most.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates the catastrophic effects of not getting enough vitamin D, meeting a young boy suffering from rickets and discovering the surprising origin of the fortified vitamin D that's added to many supermarket foods. programme.desc: Mike Bodnar and Liz Prendergast live on a houseboat in France for most of the year, but would like somewhere warmer for the winter months. programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.desc: The competition kicks off at David and Ewa Woodward's Keats Cottage in Shanklin on the Isle of Wight. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Jasmine House in Lutterworth, Leicestershire managed by Donna Taylor who believes in a no frills approach. But it doesn't take long for Martin and Sara to realise all the added extras in the room don't add up. During the afternoon Leslie and Chloe show their fighting spirit during a kickboxing lesson and all the B&Bers square up to their host.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the luxury Halliwell House in Cheshire for the third day of the contest, where owners Martin Hulme and Sara Williams charge nearly Â£500 per night. Donna is astonished at the room rates, while David and Ewa are expecting nothing but perfection, but the self-service approach at breakfast confuses the guests, leaving Martin and Sara defiant in the face of their feedback.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at Soar Chapel Guest House in Ebbw Vale, the family home of mum and daughter Lesley and Chloe Priestly. Expectations are high for this duo and one guest in particular is not disappointed. Things get going in the afternoon during a beatboxing contest, although old tensions resurface at dinner and the hosts are left to calm things down. A massive spread is laid on at breakfast, but when one guest takes issue with the cooked items, Lesley and Chloe worry what impact it will have come payment day.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. David and Ewa Woodward want to challenge Martin's criticism over the way they run their business. Next, Donna Taylor wants to find out more about the issue that caused Martin and Sara to lose sleep. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.sub-title: Chickens, Heating and a Blank Canvas
programme.desc: Dick and Angel decide they will live as self-sufficiently as they can while restoring the chateau, and invest in some chickens to provide them with eggs for breakfast every morning. However, they still have countless problems to deal with, and have only Â£20,000 left to spend on the project. The chateau is still without either heating or water, while the 59 windows are crumbling. While Dick's renovations fall behind schedule, Angel is in her element coming up with ideas for what the finished building will look like.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Mark and Candida Diacono, who have set about building a home in the shape of a plough on their 17-acre smallholding in Devon. With its complex curved roof, timber-clad exterior and steel-framed working barn, the ambitious project presents a unique technical challenge for a local builder and his team of employees, and the task proves even more intimidating when considering Mark and Candida hope to complete work on the build within seven months.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 78-year-old man has difficulty breathing after breaking several ribs by tripping over a kerb, and doctors discover that a woman is in the latter stages of pancreatic cancer when they treat her for sepsis. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Lawrence, 26, who is rushed to St George's after being hit by a car while crossing the road on a night out in Eastbourne. Meanwhile, 53-year-old Katrina is brought to A&E after having difficulty breathing and experiencing chest pains that could indicate a heart attack.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 78-year-old man has difficulty breathing after breaking several ribs by tripping over a kerb, and doctors discover that a woman is in the latter stages of pancreatic cancer when they treat her for sepsis. Elsewhere, a 32-year-old woman is treated after bruising her neck and face when she slipped on wet decking on a night out with friends.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a mother-and-son team who have gone through 20 chefs in two years at their struggling Italian restaurant in California. The adjoining bar is a triumph, but the oldest dining establishment in town needs an overhaul if the inexperienced owners are to make a profit.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Spain to meet an entrepreneur who is investing a large amount of money in an attempt to farm the critically endangered Atlantic bluefin tuna. With just one of these fish selling for a record Â£1million at auction in Tokyo, the rewards could be huge. Kate Quilton investigates why ice cream in freezers can sometimes get gritty ahead of the best before date, while Matt Tebbutt tries to find out what the difference is between the supermarkets' premium and budget range of frozen peas.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enlists the help of seamstress Lisa Comfort to help Amber and Colin create a memory quilt. However, Amber's lack of ironing skills could result in disaster.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.sub-title: Canary Islands v Isle of Wight
programme.desc: Neil Smith and his partner Steph Grzyb regularly take holidays in Fuerteventura in the Canary Islands, where Neil can play golf in guaranteed sunshine. He would love to live there full-time, but Steph would rather settle on the Isle of Wight. Jasmine Harman and Sara Damergi are on hand to offer advice on the pros and cons of either move.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Pete and Kim Smith cannot decide whether to downsize in Warwickshire or buy a holiday home in the Antequera region of Spain with a view to one day moving there permanently.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Northamptonshire, where they find night-vision goggles, an antique snooker table and an enormous America army truck.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.sub-title: Chickens, Heating and a Blank Canvas
programme.desc: Dick and Angel decide they will live as self-sufficiently as they can while restoring the chateau, and invest in some chickens to provide them with eggs for breakfast every morning. However, they still have countless problems to deal with, and have only Â£20,000 left to spend on the project. The chateau is still without either heating or water, while the 59 windows are crumbling. While Dick's renovations fall behind schedule, Angel is in her element coming up with ideas for what the finished building will look like.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Callum Hogg and Lauren Abraham's modern Park Hotel in Gloucestershire, where sisters Sue Lewis and Wendy Germann are impressed by the colourful decor, but it's not an opinion held by all. The guests are not impressed with having to pay extra for breakfast and Callum and Lauren are faced with robust feedback that leaves them wanting answers and thinking not everyone is being fair.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Trowbridge in Wiltshire to Sue's B&B, which is run by Sue Lewis and her sister, Wendy Germann. Breakfast at the communal table has its awkward moments and Sue is under pressure, having never cooked for so many before. However, it's the feedback that leaves the hosts wondering if maybe some of the guests have not been that straightforward.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Talbot in Iwerne Minster, Dorset for the third day of the competition, where hosts Erika Watt and Ellie Gill greet their guests. The rooms do not live up to their good first impression and Callum and Lauren are quick to find fault. At breakfast, tensions simmer as Wendy calls Cameron a creep, and at feedback, some of the comments send Erika and Ellie into fits of giggles.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth visit of the week is to The Old Rectory in Llangattock, south Wales, where young hotel manager Cameron Thomas likes to keep things professional. Despite wanting to find fault, Lauren and Callum are soon to admit their bridal suite is top notch, but Erika and Ellie think there may be some cleaning issues. During an afternoon of canoe racing the group members show their competitive sides.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The Park Hotel in Gloucestershire is up first as owners Callum Hogg and Lauren Abraham want to get to the bottom of why Cameron didn't sleep soundly. Next, Sue Lewis from Sue's B&B seeks to discover the deasons for Callum and Lauren's low score for the cleanliness. When all issues are resolved, the winning establishment is revealed.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple of ex-pats attempt to run a new fishing charter, two B&B owners in France face their biggest challenge yet, and on the Costa Blanca, a family pulls together to try and rescue a Spanish dream home.(n)

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: As the competition continues, the 10 remaining potters tackle raku firing, but first the pressure is on to hand-build two animal figurines and face a tricky ceramic technique. Judges Sue Pryke and Keith Brymer Jones are on hand to decide who will be crowned Potter of the Week, and who will be leaving the competition. Hosted by Melanie Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Clinton Dall from West Sussex, a self-made businessman and father of four aiming to build one of the largest homes ever featured on the show. Dall wants the end project to be perfect - from the finish of his mirrored cruciform steel columns, to how the floor tiles line up with the dining room table, and is willing to spend whatever it takes to achieve his vision. However, the construction represents a huge risk, as down one road lies abject failure and financial ruin, while down the other, is the creation of something sublime and almost heroic.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: This time, Charlie Luxton meets Tom and Georgina in Berkshire, who are planning to renovate a run-down bungalow.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets Tabitha and Chris, who have purchased 120 acres of rainforest in Toolangi and are building a farmhouse that will be completely off grid.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood.(n)

programme.desc: Clip show featuring the voice of Jake Yapp, as home videos reveal the dumbest things attempted by adults, kids and even animals.(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys visit an aviation museum that is closing down and are given their pick of the stock before looking for treasure in an art gallery's outbuildings and a barn.(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Rival teams of amateur racers pit their wacky, gravity-powered soapbox vehicles in a downhill race against the clock in Paris.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters find three small cars at an affordable price - a task that involves pestering bankers, harnessing large dogs and almost getting arrested. James May meets American rally driver Ken Block, and Jeremy Clarkson tests out the Mercedes SL Black. Comedian Michael McIntyre is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Ultimate Movers
programme.sub-title: Beer Silo, Plane Bar, Steam Train
programme.desc: A 32-metre long beer tank must navigate tiny roads to be installed at a brewery in Tadcaster, while in Kidderminster, a vintage steam train travels hundreds of miles south to Devon.(n)

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: The work taking place in one of Britain's leading police car workshops, as bespoke vehicles are built and repairs made to ones that have been out in the field. In the first episode, John Heussi finds himself in need of a new riot van, and stress-testing the old one helps him design a new one.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mail's delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuiness, Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff go on a road trip in a trio of affordable second-hand convertibles. On the test track, Chris puts the new Ariel Atom through its paces, while Freddie goes bungee jumping off a dam in an old Rover.(n)

programme.title: Dara O Briain's Go 8 Bit
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges and Natasia Demetriou play a series of video games, including Joust and Plants vs Zombies: Garden Warfare 2.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey describes how Winston Churchill drew on the lessons of his ancestor John to lead the UK through the Second World War. At two key moments, the prime minister's strategic sense helped avert disaster, and in both decisions, the influence of the Duke of Marlborough is apparent. Starkey argues that it was Winston's ability to control events by giving them a narrative meaning that enabled him to shape world history long after his fall from power.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on an expedition along Dorset's Jurassic coast to explore the county's hidden Second World War story. Starting beside the defences on Chesil Beach, he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay. On his five-day walk he encounters stunning scenery and uncovers wartime acts of ingenuity and bravery. He also discovers the crucial part a swannery played in developing a secret weapon and reveals the role of Dorset in protecting Britain from invasion during the conflict.(n)

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The mysterious death of the Duke of Kent in a 1942 plane crash, which has led to accusations of the Royal family erasing him from history.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of Major General Roy Urquhart, who successfully evaded the enemy in his retreat across the Rhine after leading British troops into a doomed attack at Arnhem.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A village ruin that is now pitted and scarred, a ghostly fleet of ships, a bridge to nowhere hidden in a forest and strange metal structures in a wood.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines animals that are able to expand their bodies, including anacondas and camels.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the unusual ways in which flamingoes and blue whales eat, examining the ways feeding patterns have influenced both species' behaviour in similar ways.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris takes a railway trip across Canada, revealing how 2,500 miles of transcontinental railway was built against huge odds in just a few decades, helping to join together a vast wilderness of isolated communities and create the country that exists today. He begins his journey on one of the earliest sections of track to be built, on a train that is now a lifeline for hunters and fishermen. He then sees one of the biggest, most famous trains in the world - the Canadian, before heading for the Prairies, and on to the port of Vancouver. Along the way he also searches for Winnie the Pooh, crosses raging rivers and meets the descendant of a pioneering Yorkshire family who arrived here more than 100 years ago.(n)

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: With the arrival of autumn the Arctic Ocean begins freezing over, and polar bears gather in large numbers on the coast as they await the return of the ice. Two thousand beluga whales head for a particular estuary to thrash against the gravel and exfoliate, while in Antarctica, Adelie penguins take to the sea for the first time and face the danger of leopard seal attacks.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey describes how Winston Churchill drew on the lessons of his ancestor John to lead the UK through the Second World War. At two key moments, the prime minister's strategic sense helped avert disaster, and in both decisions, the influence of the Duke of Marlborough is apparent. Starkey argues that it was Winston's ability to control events by giving them a narrative meaning that enabled him to shape world history long after his fall from power.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on an expedition along Dorset's Jurassic coast to explore the county's hidden Second World War story. Starting beside the defences on Chesil Beach, he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay. On his five-day walk he encounters stunning scenery and uncovers wartime acts of ingenuity and bravery. He also discovers the crucial part a swannery played in developing a secret weapon and reveals the role of Dorset in protecting Britain from invasion during the conflict.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on a 45-mile hike through the countryside of the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the area's rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps him relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. From there, he travels up what used to be secret paths to Hever Castle and finds out how the monarch's reign brought not only huge social, political and industrial change, but also fame and disaster to the women who caught his eye He ends this leg of his journey in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.(n)

As a blizzard rages, a polar bear treks into the Arctic mountains to give birth, while the entire world population of spectacled eider braves the weather in a giant ice hole. programme.desc: Ray overhears Debra's parents discussing marriage counselling when they come over for Thanksgiving. Comedy, starring Ray Romano.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: House

programme.desc: Welfare claimants for whom weight could pose a serious health issue, including 31-stone James, who rarely leaves home due to his agoraphobia, and 30-stone Kathleen, who fears her weight-related diabetes could kill her. However, a technicality arises in the case, threatening to deny them the conviction. Starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A pregnant security guard is injured when a package explodes at a scientific research lab, prompting Green and Lupo to investigate a chemist's latest experiments. It transpires that research is being undertaken to establish whether it is possible to isolate the 'gay gene' in unborn foetuses. Jesse L Martin and Jeremy Sisto star.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A dispute over a pair of trousers missing from a dry cleaner's ends in murder - and the ensuing investigation leads Lupo and Green to a businessman working for a shady multinational chain of department stores. Drama, starring Jesse L Martin and Jeremy Sisto.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green believe the fatal stabbing of psychologist Tracy Conley may be linked to confidential client information on her computer. They learn one of the files on her hard drive has been tampered with since her death - information that would have ensured a known rapist spends another five years behind bars. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his team are called in to investigate the murder of a fellow NCIS agent. Seemingly aware that he was about to be killed, the victim is known to have swallowed a memory card relating to a cold case he was working on. Tony goes undercover to glean more information - and gets the surprise of his life. Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of a US Marine and discovers three former members of his unit have recently died. Digging deeper, evidence is uncovered suggesting the commander may be a serial killer who preys on his own men. Drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Green investigate when an adulterous art dealer is stoned to death. The detectives learn that the victim's plan to display provocative art led to unwanted attention from a terrorist organisation and gives them cause to suspect her son - a fanatical Christian - was persuaded by a religious leader to kill his mother. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Green comes under investigation by Internal Affairs after he shoots a suspect, and the case reveals a host of secrets the detective would rather keep hidden, threatening not only his police career but his freedom. Jesse L Martin stars, alongside S Epatha Merkerson and Linus Roache.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy Seal falls to his death while training for a secret mission and his commanding officer believes it was an accident. Analysis of the victim's equipment proves it was sabotaged, but fingerprints at the crime scene reveal nothing, prompting the agents to re-enact the sequence of events before the time allocated for the assignment runs out.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs faces an old adversary who has re-emerged with a plan to assassinate the US president and taken Kate hostage in a bid to ensure success. The FBI manages to foil the attack on the presidential helicopter, Marine One, but the case seems set to end on a downbeat note. Rudolf Martin guest stars, with Mark Harmon and Sasha Alexander.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Logan begins to investigate the murder of a police recruit, but the death of a casual girlfriend leaves him too distracted to concentrate, and he bows out of the case. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates the ritualistic murder of a boy, a woman and an elderly man, and finds they all figured in sexual abuse cases investigated by Benson. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

As Benson and Stabler set about piecing together the previous night's events, two fraternity boys become the main suspects. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy captain is held hostage in his own office while his wife and blind daughter are abducted, and the kidnappers try to extort a large sum of money from him for his family's release. The NCIS team is soon on the case, but initially struggles to identify the chief assailant.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs investigates the discovery of a woman's mummified remains, following a trail of clues that takes him and the team to Florida, where they search for another victim who mysteriously disappeared. One of their few leads is a manual on the secret of being a good wife - which eventually points them in the direction of a serial killer.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine helicopter is discovered abandoned in a crop circle with its pilots missing, and witnesses report seeing strange lights in the night sky, adding to the intrigue surrounding the case. Gibbs orders the sightings to be kept under wraps for fear of attracting the attention of UFO enthusiasts, before uncovering a dark secret involving local residents. Guest starring Timothy Bottoms (The Last Picture Show).(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The kidnapping of a baby at a grocery store leads the detectives to a shady racket that involves snatching infants and arranging to sell them for adoption. Unexpectedly, the investigation produces new leads on an old case involving a murdered mother and her missing child - which had been supervised by Cragen and his partner. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An HIV-positive man with a record of assaulting under-age girls is suspected of a series of rapes and murders - but Stabler's determination to charge him results in tragedy. Undeterred, the team pursues its new suspect, a rap promoter, though his cunning leaves little hope of a conviction. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An exceptional teacher who helped inner-city kids get into college is murdered, but Stabler and Benson's investigation reveals her sinister double life as a sex addict - which has made her plenty of enemies. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads.
